analysis:
1. Regime Change Odds Collapsing on Near-Term Horizons

The market on Iranian regime falling by March 31 sits at just 4%, down 2.75pp this week and 6.65pp over the month — the single highest-volume market in the dataset at $33M. The June 30 window is priced at 29%, also down 6pp this week. The end-of-2026 horizon holds at 37%, off 3pp this week. The directional message is unambiguous: markets are consistently repricing regime change as less likely across every timeframe, with the sharpest compression on the shortest windows.

2. US Military Action — Probability Eroding Across the Board

"US forces enter Iran by December 31" is at 65% but dropped 13.5pp this week — the largest absolute weekly move in the dataset. The US invasion by March 31 sits at 15.1%, down 3pp on the week. The full-year invasion market ("Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027") is at 46%, off 1.5pp this week. US formal war declaration by March 31 is a near-zero 1%. The broad signal: the market is pulling back hard from imminent or near-term US military entry scenarios.

3. Diplomacy and Nuclear Deal — The One Area Gaining Ground

"US-Iran nuclear deal by June 30" is at 21%, up 5pp this week and up 13pp over the month — the strongest upward momentum in the dataset. "Iran agrees to end uranium enrichment by March 31" ticked up 2pp this week to 3.9%. The US-Iran ceasefire by June 30 is priced at 60%, and by May 31 at 51%, though neither showed week-change data. The nuclear deal market's 13pp monthly surge is a standout signal that diplomatic resolution is being increasingly priced in relative to military outcomes.

4. Oil Price Shock Risk — High Baseline Expectations

Markets price a 85.2% chance crude hits $100 by end of March, 76.5% for $105, and 63.9% for $110. The $120 strike is at 43% and $150 at 14%. The $200 scenario sits at 3.3%. None of these markets showed weekly change data, suggesting stable conviction rather than reactive trading. The baseline expectation of oil above $100 by month-end reflects the broader market context of active US-Iran military operations being priced in at significant probability.

5. Key Shifts This Week

"US forces enter Iran by December 31" fell 13.5pp to 65% — the largest weekly move across all markets and a clear signal that traders are reducing exposure to a prolonged US military presence scenario. "Will the Iranian regime fall by June 30" dropped 6pp to 29%, extending a consistent downward trend. "US-Iran nuclear deal by June 30" gained 5pp to 21%, its momentum now at +13pp over the month — the clearest directional trade in the dataset. "Reza Pahlavi enter Iran by March 31" fell 2.2pp to just 2.1%, reinforcing that regime transition scenarios are being heavily discounted. "Will France, UK, or Germany strike Iran by March 31" dropped 2.75pp to 8.1%, consistent with Europe being priced out of direct military involvement.
